rationale: The cited paper is used as a key empirical benchmark from the national-brand umbrella-branding literature, not just as a background list citation. In the introduction, the citing paper explicitly summarizes prior findings (including Erdem and Sun 2002) on intrinsic brand strength and marketing-mix effectiveness, then motivates its own study as testing whether those results generalize to private-label umbrella rebranding. In the discussion, it directly contrasts its results with Erdem and Sun (2002), especially on price-promotion effectiveness, indicating a meaningful interpretive role for this specific citation.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Erdem and Sun (2002) by examining umbrella-branding effects in a new context (retailer private labels rebranded across many categories). It shows partial carryover and partial divergence from the earlier national-brand findings, thereby specifying when prior umbrella-branding effects do and do not generalize.
